Caption: General block diagram of RECIF installation.
Photographs of RECIF site (installation of PV panels is ongoing). Site geolocation: 17°34′34.2″S 149°36′33.7″W.
Isometric view of RECIF site once the solar panels and the corresponding structure will be installed.

Photographs of container open and close spaces: (1) Fuel cell (2) Electrolyzer (3) Dryer (4) Thermochemical reactor (5) Extractor #1 (6) H2 transmitter #1 (7) NH3 transmitter (8) H2 transmitter #2 (9) Automatic fire extinguisher (10) Water demineralizer (11) Bottom air vent (12) Top air vent (13) 100L distilled water tank (14) Inverter (15) CAB-3 cabinet(∗) (16) Transformer(∗) (17) Cooling water system (18) Air conditioner(∗∗) (19) Visual alarm (20) Extractor #2 (21) Thermochemical prototype (22) Network rack (23) PC with SCADA (24) CAB-2 cabinet, exterior (25) 1 m3 water tank (26) H2 tank (27) H2 purges (28) H2 distribution. (∗)See electrical diagram in Ref. [46]. (∗∗) Does not correspond to the thermochemical unit; used to acclimatize the room when the thermochemical prototype is not in operation.
P&ID, hydrogen storage and distribution.
P&ID, water storage and demineralization to feed the electrolyzer.
P&ID, electrolyzer & dryer.
P&ID, hydrogen fuel cell.
P&ID, fuel cell and electrolyzer cooling system & thermochemical prototype for cooling production and storage.
ATEX zones definition of RECIF installation.
Electricity and hydrogen supply to container.

“CAB-2” electrical cabinet that contains the safety PLC; it is installed on the front outside wall of container. Some of the components coding (e.g. -S.2.40, -PF.2.14) can also be found in Figs. 11, Figs. 14 and 15.
Permissible operating modes of the inverter. The arrows correspond to the direction of power flow.

Block diagram of the logic solver of the SIS implemented in the safety PLC. Note that a logic ‘1’ represents a normal condition and ‘0’ a triggered condition (for the inputs, outputs and internal states).
Block diagram of the logic solver that implements SIF #3. Note that a logic ‘1’ represents a normal condition and ‘0’ a triggered condition.

Extract of the Descriptive System Document (DSD) of all the ATEX components with intrinsically safe mode of protection (see IEC 60079-25 [43], §4 and Annex E). The complete DSD can be found in Ref. [46] (in French).
Risk graph of SIF #1a and #1b (image extracted from SISTEMA [49] project).
Reliability block diagram of SIF #1a and #1b. Left: graphical representation. Right: SISTEMA [49] project tree structure.

Model of a leak process inside the container used to simulate the evolution of gas concentration. After detection of a gas leak, the safety system will turn on the extractors and the quantity of gas trapped inside the equipment will progressively leave the container.
Simplified block diagram of the model of a leak of a reservoir at initial pressure P 0 into the container.

Simulation results of a gas leak process inside the container. (a) Fuel cell leak that starts at 0:30 and a minute later the concentration reaches 10% of LFL. (b) Electrolyzer leak that starts at 0:30 and at 04:00 the concentration reaches 10% of LFL. On both cases, once the concentration reaches the alarm level, the safety system turns off the fuel cell and electrolyzer, closes the fuel cell H2 supply and raise the ventilation flow rate.
